data.title: Positive Outlook for Adverum Biotechnologies: Buy Rating Based on Promising ixo-vec Therapy and Strategic Financing Plans
data.description:
Joseph Thome has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including the promising progress of Adverum Biotechnologies’ ixo-vec therapy for wet AMD. The company has completed screening for its Phase III ARTEMIS study ahead of schedule, which suggests strong demand and a positive outlook for patient enrollment.
Additionally, the study’s design, which includes both treatment-naive and previously treated patients, is expected to provide a comprehensive understanding of ixo-vec’s efficacy, potentially offering advantages over competitors. Furthermore, despite concerns about funding, management’s confidence in securing non-dilutive financing options such as royalty transactions or partnerships supports the potential for future growth, making the stock appear undervalued at its current price.

Thome covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as United Therapeutics, PTC Therapeutics, and uniQure. According to TipRanks, Thome has an average return of 5.3% and a 51.24% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Mizuho Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $12.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Banco BPM: Strategic Diversification and Anima Acquisition Drive Strong Growth Potential
data.description:
Chris Hallam has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Banco BPM’s strong growth potential and revenue resilience. The bank has strategically diversified its revenue streams, which helps mitigate risks associated with declining interest rates. This shift in focus from net interest income to fee and commission income is expected to enhance revenue stability.
Additionally, the acquisition of Anima is projected to decrease the proportion of net interest income to around 50% and increase fee income to 45% by 2025. These changes, along with effective cost management initiatives, are anticipated to drive above-average growth and returns. Consequently, Hallam has set a 12-month price target of €15.7, suggesting a potential upside of approximately 25%.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Benitec Biopharma’s Strong Financial Position and Promising Clinical Progress Justify Buy Rating
data.description:
Ritu Baral has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors surrounding Benitec Biopharma’s financial and clinical progress. The company has a strong cash position of $98 million, which is projected to sustain operations through 2027. This financial stability supports the ongoing Phase 1b/2a trial and the anticipated start of a registrational trial in the second half of 2026.
Additionally, the company’s investigational therapy, BB-301, is advancing in its clinical trials for OPMD dysphagia, with promising interim data expected in the fourth quarter of 2025. The study involves both objective and subjective measures, which will provide comprehensive insights into the therapy’s efficacy. These factors, combined with strategic financial management and promising clinical data, underpin the Buy rating recommendation.

In another report released today, Citizens JMP also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $20.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
